title: Geospatial Analysis and Spatial Evidence Policy
capabilities: user-defined bounding-box, polygon, and radius areas of interest
geographic counts and numeric aggregation without imputation

methods.description: Point-in-polygon, segment intersection, and representative-point radius tests are applied to WGS84 GeoJSON.
Point distances use the haversine formula. Non-point features use a clearly labeled minimum vertex/centroid approximation.
Only intersecting features are counted or aggregated. Missing metric values are reported and never imputed.
Stable feature identifiers are compared across immutable dataset versions; geometry and property digests reveal changes.

limitations: No coordinate reprojection is performed; inputs must use EPSG:4326 longitude and latitude.
The dependency-light engine is not a replacement for cadastral, surveying, emergency-response, or high-precision GIS software.
Non-point proximity is approximate and does not calculate exact geodesic distance to every edge.
Spatial overlap, proximity, or temporal coincidence does not establish causation, responsibility, severity, or individual risk.
responsible_use: Do not use this studio for military targeting, weapons deployment, or operational tracking of people.
Do not create precise personal-location profiles or infer protected characteristics from spatial records.
Public outputs must preserve source, date, method, resolution, license, and limitation context.
Human review is required before publishing consequential spatial claims.
